title: Dawn Meadow
tileHint: A meadow in the first cold light of morning, where frost, mist, and waking creatures shape the encounter

1.hint: Move quietly through the cold light and let the meadow reveal its shy keeper.
1.successTitle: A meadow creature trusts your stillness
1.successBody: $companionName chooses your road as though dawn itself had approved of you.
1.failureBody: You see the waking life of the meadow, but it slips back into distance before trust can form.

2.hint: Meet the waking world with warmth, care, and no demand for reward.
2.successTitle: The first light leaves you a friend
2.successBody: $companionName remains beside you, drawn by the warmth you carried into the cold morning.
2.failureTitle: Kindness is felt, but not kept
2.failureBody: The little life you comforted leaves better than you found it, though it does not follow.

3.hint: Answer the morning's danger quickly and without hesitation.
3.successTitle: A fierce morning bond is made
3.successBody: $companionName stays with the one who stood against danger before the sun was fully risen.
3.failureTitle: The rescue holds, but the bond does not
3.failureBody: Your courage changes the morning for the better, yet the creature keeps its freedom rather than its company.

startText: Dawn lies cold over the meadow. Frost clings to the lower grass, mist drifts close to the ground, and every living thing seems caught between sleep and waking.

start.text: The meadow at dawn answers each traveler differently. In the half-light, caution, kindness, and boldness do not lead to the same creature, or the same kind of trust.

choices.label: Walk softly through the frost and listen before acting
Offer warmth and care to whatever the morning reveals
Go at once toward the first sign of danger

watch1.text: You keep your tread light on the silvered grass and let the waking meadow grow accustomed to your presence before you ask anything from it.

choices.label: Follow the fine tracks at the edge of the mist
Stand still until the small sounds return around you

kind1.text: The cold hour before full sunrise is hardest on the smallest lives. You begin by noticing who among them most needs gentleness rather than haste.

choices.label: Share what little warmth you carry
Shield the frailest creature the dawn has left exposed

bold1.text: The waking world is not always gentle. Even in dawn's beauty there are hawks, traps, and the quick violence of hunger.

choices.label: Rush in before the danger can finish its work
Strike hard and trust courage to settle the rest

final.text: By the time the meadow has fully woken, it has already decided what kind of company, if any, your road deserves.

commitChoices.label: Move quietly and let dawn's shy creature choose you
Offer warmth and care to the exposed life of the morning
Step into danger before the sun can burn the frost away
